steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the year 2000 data
steps.content: In year 2000 ($t = 0$), the production was 4 million barrels. For a linear function $f(t) = mt + b$, we have $f(0) = 4$, so $b = 4$. All four choices have this same y-intercept, so we need to determine the slope.

steps.title: Calculate the slope from the production change
steps.content:
The production fell from 4 million barrels at $t = 0$ to 1.9 million barrels at $t = 13$ (year 2013). Using the slope formula:
$$m = \frac{1.9 - 4}{13 - 0} = \frac{-2.1}{13}$$
To express in the form shown in the answer choices, multiply both numerator and denominator by 10:
$$\frac{-2.1}{13} = \frac{-21}{130}$$
Therefore, $f(t) = -\frac{21}{130}t + 4$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
